name: Source Fidelity review for Telomere Protection And Capping bundle
summary: The staged source, study, and finding records are source-faithful for the cited PubMed and PMC records. PMIDs, DOIs, venues, publication years, population/model descriptions, sample sizes where staged, endpoint categories, quantitative notes, and caveats match the checked source metadata, abstracts, and full-text passages closely enough for editorial approval.

findings.claim_or_issue: The AAV9-TRF1 source supports improved mouse healthspan markers and reduced short-telomere/telomere-DNA-damage signals in some tissues, while median lifespan was not significantly changed.
findings.why_it_matters: This is the strongest direct intervention signal in the bundle, so keeping it as healthspan and biomarker evidence prevents overstatement.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the staged wording focused on mouse healthspan and telomere-protection markers, not median lifespan extension.

findings.claim_or_issue: The human arterial uncapping source supports age-associated arterial biomarker differences in 104 donors and a link to P53/P21 senescence markers independent of mean telomere length.
findings.why_it_matters: The source does not test an intervention, so it should support human target relevance rather than a human treatment stage.
findings.recommended_action: Retain the staged caveats that this is observational tissue biomarker evidence and does not identify a capping-directed therapy.

findings.claim_or_issue: The hypertension source supports age-matched associative biomarker differences: greater arterial uncapping and P53/P21 signaling in hypertensive participants without a significant mean telomere-length difference.
findings.why_it_matters: The source does not show that changing telomere capping treats or prevents hypertension.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the staged disease-context caveat and do not treat this source as therapeutic evidence.

findings.claim_or_issue: The D+Q source supports an aged-mouse senolytic reduction in endothelial DNA damage and TIF markers, with 10 mice per group treated from 21 to 24 months, but the mechanism is senescent-cell clearance rather than direct shelterin or cap repair.
findings.why_it_matters: Counting D+Q as a direct telomere-capping therapy would overstate the specificity of the intervention.
findings.recommended_action: Retain the staged wording that treats this as an adjacent signal with mechanism and species limitations.

findings.claim_or_issue: The TRF2 overexpression source supports the staged safety boundary: skin-directed TRF2 overexpression in mice produced telomere loss, DNA damage, premature skin deterioration, hyperpigmentation, and increased skin cancer.
findings.why_it_matters: This source calibrates against a simple more-shelterin-is-better interpretation.
findings.recommended_action: Keep this boundary visible in the outlook support map and safety caveats.
